solution.rawText:
Multiplying out the brackets does not look like a particularly useful approach (what would we
do with the terms cos x and sin x cos x in the same inequality?), so we will leave the expression
in its factored form.


If we temporarily write a = 1 Рѕњ 2 sin x and b = cos x we will be able to see the structure of the
problem a little more clearly. We want ab РЕЙ 0, and this will happen when either a РЕЙ 0 and
b РЕЙ 0, or when a РЕй 0 and b РЕй 0. So we need to solve each of these simpler inequalities.


We have 1 Рѕњ 2 sin x = 0 when sin x = 21 , so when x = ¤ђ6 or x = 5¤ђ  6 . We can either sketch the
graph of y = 1 Рѕњ 2 sin x or try values to determine whether 1 Рѕњ 2 sin x is positive or negative
in each range. Sketching the graph seems possibly hard, as there are multiple transformations,
and it would be easy to make a mistake. So on this occasion, weРђЎll plug in values: x = 0 and
x = ¤ђ both give 1 Рѕњ 2 sin x = 1; while x = ¤ђ2 gives 1 Рѕњ 2 sin x = Рѕњ1. Therefore


                     1 Рѕњ 2 sin x РЕЙ 0 when 0 РЕй x РЕй ¤ђ6 and when 5¤ђ
                                                               6 РЕйxРЕй¤ђ
                     1 Рѕњ 2 sin x РЕй 0 when ¤ђ6 РЕй x РЕй 5¤ђ
                                                    6


We can do similarly for cos x; this time we can think about the graph in our head, and we have



                                   cos x РЕЙ 0 when 0 РЕй x РЕй ¤ђ2
                                   cos x РЕй 0 when ¤ђ2 РЕй x РЕй ¤ђ

We can now put everything together. Both expressions are greater than or equal to 0 when
0 РЕй x РЕй ¤ђ6 , and they are both less than or equal to 0 when ¤ђ2 РЕй x РЕй 5¤ђ
                                                                      6 .
Hence the answer is A.
